rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and insert only an AGREE annex block within a valid owner scope.
Keep contract-style assumptions and guarantees in component type scope; keep implementation-level bindings only in implementation scope.
Use the analyzed model as the authority for valid owners, visible features, and implementation contents before attaching any annex.
generic_rules: Do not move, rename, or alter unrelated architectural elements when adding an annex.
Respect owner boundaries: type-level contracts must not rely on implementation-only paths or subcomponent references.
If required scope or visible symbols are not confirmed by the model structure, avoid speculative placement or cross-scope content.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scope annex content should appear in a valid implementation owner and after relevant structural declarations when scope depends on them.
anti_patterns: Do not place type-scope contract content into an implementation owner unless it is truly implementation-scoped.
Do not fuse annex content in a way that changes connections, features, properties, or other existing architecture.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE expression variables
